generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (PMSM) driving the first vertical robot axis via gear transmission"
]

instance GearboxActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox reducing motor speed to achieve precise motion control for the robot's first vertical axis"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Absolute encoder mounted directly on the motor to measure motor angle and velocity in real-time"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorAngleVelocityData
]

instance GearboxEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Absolute encoder positioned behind the gearbox to measure gearbox output angle and velocity"
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxAngleVelocityData
]

instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring input current (I_M) from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D controller via controller interface"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InputCurrentData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorAngleVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance GearboxAngleVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxEncoder
]

instance InputCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
]

instance DerivedTorqueData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InputCurrentData
    base:desc "Derived torque values (τ_M) calculated from input current (I_M) using motor-specific constants"
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box friction model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction characteristics for motor-gearbox system"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulinkEnabler
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box friction model implementing Stribeck friction characteristics for high-speed applications"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulinkEnabler
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box friction model implementing LuGre friction characteristics with dynamic stiffness representation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulinkEnabler
]

instance MotorGearboxStructureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) describing the structural components of the first vertical robot axis (motor, gearbox, cycloidal gearbox)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler
]

instance RobotDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model implementing robot-specific dynamics for deriving reference torque/velocity outputs"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulinkEnabler
]

instance MeasuredDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-stamped collection of physical measurements (motor angle/velocity, gearbox angle/velocity, input current, derived torque)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorAngleVelocityData, GearboxAngleVelocityData, InputCurrentData, DerivedTorqueData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTProtocolEnabler
]

instance SimulatedDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Reference outputs (motor output torque/velocity) and friction-affected outputs for comparison with measured data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RobotDynamicsModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CustomDashboardEnabler
]

instance FrictionLossData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Calculated friction loss metrics (static, kinetic, viscous) derived from torque and velocity measurements"
    DTDFVocab:fromData DerivedTorqueData, MotorAngleVelocityData, GearboxAngleVelocityData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CustomDashboardEnabler
]

instance SensorMetadata : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Metadata tracking source tracing (MQTT channels, sensor IDs) and time-stamping for data provenance"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MQTTProtocolEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTProtocolEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling tool (Block Definition Diagram and Internal Block Diagram) for structural modeling of robot components"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MotorGearboxStructureModel
]

instance MATLABSimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink environment for simulation modeling (friction models, dynamics models)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel, LuGreFrictionModel, RobotDynamicsModel
]

instance MQTTProtocolEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation for real-time sensor data streaming from SINUMERIK 840D controller"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MeasuredDataTrace, SensorMetadata
]

instance CustomDashboardEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom visualization dashboard integrating friction-related data and model outputs"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionLossData, SimulatedDataTrace
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time identification and refinement of white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) friction models"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCharacterizationInsight, ModelRefinementAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integrated visualization of friction-related data (torque, angular velocity, friction loss) for operational monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionVisualizationInsight, AnomalyDetectionAction
]

instance ControlOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Model-based control optimization using simulation models to adjust motion accuracy by dynamically updating friction models"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlParameterAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionCharacterizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time identification of static, kinetic, and viscous friction components in the gearbox system"
]

instance ModelRefinementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic updating of friction models (white-box/black-box hybrid approaches) based on live data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FrictionVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of friction loss vs. simulated behavior for operational monitoring"
]

instance AnomalyDetectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Detection of anomalies (wear, misalignment) through comparison of measured vs. simulated friction data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlParameterAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjustment of motion accuracy parameters based on friction model updates and simulation comparisons"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Environment
instance RobotFirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MotorActuator, GearboxActuator, MotorEncoder, GearboxEncoder, CurrentSensor
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MotorGearboxStructureModel, CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel, LuGreFrictionModel,
    RobotDynamicsModel, MeasuredDataTrace, SimulatedDataTrace, FrictionLossData, SensorMetadata,
    MATLABSimulinkEnabler, CustomDashboardEnabler, FrictionAnalysisService, VisualizationService, ControlOptimizationService
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is **real-time and dynamic**, with:
   - **Data sampling frequency**: Sensor data (e.g., motor current, encoders) is streamed continuously or in near-real-time (not explicitly quantified but implied to be high-frequency for friction analysis).
   - **Model updates**: Continuous refinement of friction models during operation, enabling adaptive control adjustments based on live data.
   - **Synchronization**: Aligned with the robot’s operational cycle (e.g., machining tasks), ensuring friction models are updated in sync with physical system dynamics."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is **single-axis focused** for the robot’s first vertical axis, but leverages:
   - **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)**: To standardize and link digital shadow components (e.g., motor, gearbox models) with physical assets.
   - **Hierarchical structure**: Combines structural (SysML) and behavioral (Simulink) models to enable multi-physics awareness (e.g., coupling friction models with torque/velocity data).
   - **No explicit mention of decentralized/twin-based orchestration**, but the methodology supports modular extension to other axes."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates across the **operational lifecycle** of the robot, specifically:
   - **As-operated phase**: Focuses on **real-time friction analysis** during machining tasks to improve motion accuracy.
   - **No explicit stages for design/manufacture**, but the methodology supports integration of engineering models (e.g., SysML) for future lifecycle phases."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are addressed through:
   - **Model validation**: Comparison of simulated vs. measured data (e.g., friction-affected vs. frictionless outputs) to validate model accuracy.
   - **Uncertainty handling**: Use of dual-encoder arrangement (motor/gearbox encoders) to reduce positional uncertainty and backlash effects.
   - **Verification**: Real-time processing pipeline (e.g., MQTT + simulation models) ensures data integrity and model traceability.
   - **No explicit mention of formal validation frameworks**, but the methodology emphasizes semantic alignment and traceability."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical and virtual systems uses:
   - **Network protocol**: MQTT for real-time data streaming from the robot’s CNC controller (SINUMERIK 840D sl) to the digital twin.
   - **Data formats**: Sensor data (e.g., motor current, angles) and derived values (e.g., torque) are transmitted as floating-point arrays with timestamps.
   - **Interoperability**: Asset Administration Shells (AAS) enable standardized communication between physical assets and digital models."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital twin is deployed as:
   - **Real-time processing**: Live data is processed on-demand via MATLAB Simulink models and visualized in a custom dashboard.
   - **No explicit mention of cloud/edge deployment**, but the methodology supports distributed processing for scalability.
   - **Hardware**: Likely hosted on a local server or edge device connected to the robot’s CNC controller."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The digital twin integrates horizontally with:
   - **External systems**: Likely linked to the robot’s CNC controller (SINUMERIK 840D sl) for data exchange.
   - **Standardized protocols**: Asset Administration Shells (AAS) enable interoperability with other digital assets or manufacturing systems.
   - **No explicit mention of external DTs or enterprise systems**, but the methodology supports modular extension."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin adheres to:
   - **Modeling standards**: SysML for structural/behavioral modeling and Asset Administration Shells (AAS) for interoperability.
   - **Simulation standards**: MATLAB Simulink for executable friction models.
   - **Data standards**: MQTT protocol for real-time data streaming.
   - **No explicit mention of industry-specific standards** (e.g., IEC 62264), but the methodology supports compliance with MBSE frameworks."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The digital twin includes:
   - **Data security**: MQTT protocol with potential encryption (not specified) for secure sensor data transmission.
   - **Safety**: Real-time processing ensures no autonomous intervention in the physical system (digital shadow is passive).
   - **No explicit mention of human-robot interaction safety**, but the methodology emphasizes passive data collection for operational monitoring."
]
